raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The artifact closes with an explicit contribution claim ('first calibration of expert detection accuracy on a real-world OLTP workload'); validating whether that claim is substantiated by the described study is a natural central check regardless of interpretation.","R1.2":"The causal inference chain — pre/post accuracy improvement attributed to a training intervention — and the mixed-effects modelling choice are internally auditable; logical consistency here is likely central to any scientific check.","R1.3":"Precise quantitative claims (52.8%, 71.4%, κ ≈ 0.41, 95% CIs, p < 0.001) are anchored to a described experimental design; checking whether those numbers are coherent with the described methodology and support the stated conclusions is a prominent check target."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I focus on scientific/analytical validity — e.g., statistical correctness, experimental-design soundness, evidence-claim alignment — or on writing and editorial quality — e.g., prose clarity, grammar, and style?"}

rationale.R1.1: The artifact closes with an explicit contribution claim ('first calibration of expert detection accuracy on a real-world OLTP workload'); validating whether that claim is substantiated by the described study is a natural central check regardless of interpretation.
rationale.R1.2: The causal inference chain — pre/post accuracy improvement attributed to a training intervention — and the mixed-effects modelling choice are internally auditable; logical consistency here is likely central to any scientific check.
rationale.R1.3: Precise quantitative claims (52.8%, 71.4%, κ ≈ 0.41, 95% CIs, p < 0.001) are anchored to a described experimental design; checking whether those numbers are coherent with the described methodology and support the stated conclusions is a prominent check target.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on scientific/analytical validity — e.g., statistical correctness, experimental-design soundness, evidence-claim alignment — or on writing and editorial quality — e.g., prose clarity, grammar, and style?
